build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player builds port structures (docks, tavern, warehouse, defenses) on a coastal grid by spending gold. Each structure has a cost and a visible effect on the port. Score 0 if there is no building mechanic, if structures are free, or if the port cannot be expanded. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Pirate crews arrive based on port reputation. The player sends crews on raids by selecting targets from a trade route map. Raids return gold based on target value. A notoriety meter increases with each raid. Score 0 if there are no raids, if crews do not arrive, or if notoriety does not exist. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

When notoriety reaches thresholds, the navy attacks the port. Navy raids test the port's defenses (cannons, walls). Surviving reduces notoriety; failing destroys buildings and crews. Score 0 if notoriety has no consequence or if navy attacks never occur. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A gold economy drives the port: gold comes from raids, is spent on buildings and crew upkeep. A gold counter is permanently visible. Running out of gold prevents building and causes crew desertion. Score 0 if there is no gold system or if everything is free. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The full loop connects: title -> new port -> build/raid/defend cycle -> port thrives or falls -> result screen. The game ends when the port is destroyed by navy or reaches a prosperity milestone. Score 0 if any transition is missing or if there is no end condition. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least five building types exist (dock, tavern, warehouse, cannon tower, shipyard) with different sprites and functions. Score 0 if fewer than four types exist or if types are visually identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Multiple raid targets exist with different risk/reward profiles. Richer targets yield more gold but raise notoriety faster. At least three target types are available. Score 0 if only one target exists or if all targets are identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Crew types or ship types vary in combat strength, speed, and upkeep cost. Better crews require better taverns or docks to attract. Score 0 if all crews are identical or if port quality has no effect on recruitment. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Navy attacks escalate in strength as notoriety grows. Later attacks bring more ships or stronger vessels. Defenses can be upgraded to match. Score 0 if navy attacks are always the same strength or if defenses cannot be improved. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Crew morale is affected by tavern quality, raid success, and pay. Low morale causes desertion. The player must invest in crew happiness alongside military strength. Score 0 if morale does not exist or if it has no gameplay effect. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

The HUD shows gold, notoriety meter, crew count, and reputation in clear non-overlapping regions. Navy attack warnings are visible before they arrive. Score 0 if gold, notoriety, or crew info is missing. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The port view clearly shows the coastal grid, placed buildings with distinct appearances, docked ships, and the surrounding sea. Build menu and play area are separated. Score 0 if buildings are indistinguishable or if the layout is unreadable. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Actions produce visible feedback: building shows structures appear, raids show ships departing and returning, navy attacks show combat, and notoriety changes are indicated. Score 0 if actions produce no observable change. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Overall art coherence: port buildings, ships, sea, tropical island, UI, and effects belong to a consistent Caribbean-pirate visual language with warm tropical tones. Score 0 if the screen mixes incompatible styles or reads as plain default Godot grey. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Building sprites are real illustrated or pixel-art structures (wooden docks, thatched taverns, stone towers). Ships are illustrated vessels with sails and hulls. Score 0 if buildings or ships are plain colored shapes. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

The island uses real textured terrain (sand, palm trees, rocky cliffs) with a sea backdrop. The coastal setting is visually established. Score 0 if the island is a flat colored area or if there is no sea. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI use styled themed widgets with pirate chrome: gold display has coin/skull icons, notoriety has a wanted-poster style, and menus have weathered-wood backgrounds. Score 0 if UI elements are unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and atmosphere: waves lap at docks, ships rock at anchor, cannon fire produces smoke, buildings show activity (tavern lights, warehouse loading), and the port has ambient tropical life. Score 0 if all elements are static. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
